din: noun. A loud noise; a cacophony or loud commotion.
dint: noun. Especially in by dint of: force, power.
dirty: adjective. Unclean; covered with or containing unpleasant substances such as dirt or grime.
dit: verb. To stop up; block (an opening); close.
dry: adjective. Free from or lacking moisture.
duit: noun. Alternative form of doit (“a small Dutch coin”)
duits: noun. Alternative form of doits; plural of duit
dunt: noun. A stroke; a dull-sounding blow.
durst: verb. simple past and past participle of dare
duty: noun. That which one is morally or legally obligated to do.
industry: noun. The tendency to work persistently.
nit: noun. The egg of a louse.
nudist: noun. A person who practices nudism.
nudity: noun. The state or quality of being without clothing on the body; specifically, the quality of being without clothing on the genitals.
nut: noun. Any of various hard-shelled seeds or hard, dry fruits from various families of plants.
rid: verb. Followed by of: to free (oneself or someone, or a place) from an annoyance or hindrance.
rids: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of rid
rins: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of rin
ruin: noun. The remains of a destroyed or dilapidated construction, such as a house or castle.
runt: noun. The smallest animal of a litter.
runty: adjective. Having the characteristics of a runt; small and stunted; diminutive.
rust: noun. The deteriorated state of iron or steel as a result of moisture and oxidation; it consists mostly of iron(III) oxide (ferric oxide)
rusty: adjective. Marked or corroded by rust.
rut: noun. Sexual desire in any of many mammals, often specific to mating season.
rutin: noun. A flavonoid, found in many plants, that is a glycoside of quercetin and rutinose.
rynd: noun. A piece of iron crossing the hole in the upper millstone, by which the stone is supported on the spindle.
sin: noun. A violation of divine will or religious law.
sir: noun. A man of a higher rank or position.
sit: verb. To be in a position in which the upper body is upright and supported by the buttocks.
snit: noun. A temper; a bad mood.
sri: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Siriano.
stir: verb. To disturb the relative position of the particles (of a liquid or similar) by passing an object through it.
stud: noun. A male animal, especially a stud horse (stallion), kept for breeding.
study: verb. To review materials already learned in order to make sure one does not forget them, usually in preparation for an examination.
stun: verb. To incapacitate; especially by inducing disorientation or unconsciousness.
sturdy: adjective. Of firm build; stiff; stout; strong.
sty: noun. A pen or enclosure for swine.
suint: noun. A substance obtained from the dried perspiration of sheep wool, it is of a fatty and earthy consistency and contains potassium salts.
suit: noun. A set of clothes to be worn together, now especially a man's matching jacket and trousers (also business suit or lounge suit), or a
sun: noun. A star, especially when seen as the centre of any single solar system.
sundry: adjective. More than one or two but not very many; a number of, several.
surd: noun. An irrational number, especially one expressed using the √ symbol.
syn: adjective. That has a torsion angle between 0° and 90°.
tidy: adjective. Arranged neatly and in order.
tin: noun. A malleable, ductile, metallic element, resistant to corrosion, with atomic number 50 and symbol Sn.
tui: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tupuri.
tun: noun. A large cask; an oblong vessel bulging in the middle, like a pipe or puncheon, and girt with hoops; a wine cask. (See a diagram
turd: noun. A piece of solid feces.
turn: verb. To make a non-linear physical movement.
turns: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of turn
unit: noun. Oneness, singularity, seen as a component of a whole number; a magnitude of one.
unity: noun. Oneness: the state or fact of being one undivided entity.
urn: noun. A vase with a footed base.
yid: noun. Alternative letter-case form of Yid.
yin: noun. A principle in Chinese and related East Asian philosophies associated with dark, cool, female, etc. elements of the natural world.
yurt: noun. A large, round, semi-permanent tent with vertical walls and a conical roof, usually associated with Central Asia and Mongolia (where
dur: adjective. Major; in the major mode.
isn: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Isanzu.
ist: noun. A practitioner or supporter of an ism.
itd: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Southern Tidung.
nid: noun. Alternative form of nide (“nest of pheasants”).
nis: noun. A nix; a hobgoblin, especially one that resides in a farm house.
rindy: adjective. Having a rind or skin.
ritsu: noun. An anhemitonic pentatonic scale used in a Japanese Buddhist chant, and built up by intervals of major second, minor third, major
ruds: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of rud
rynt: verb. stand off; move away (said by milkmaids to their cows after milking them)
situ: noun. Only used in in situ.
snirt: verb. To give a suppressed laugh or sharp intake of breath.
strid: noun. A place where a chasm or gorge is narrow enough to be crossed.
strind: noun. A ditch or water - channel.
sud: noun. A bubble of lather or foam (the singular of suds).
suid: noun. Any of the family Suidae: a pig or a taxonomically related animal.
sundri: noun. Alternative form of sundari (“wind instrument”).
suni: noun. A small antelope of Southern Africa, Nesotragus moschatus.
sur: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Mwaghavul.
syrt: noun. A kind of elevated flatland in Russia and Central Asia.
tdr: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Todrah.
trs: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Chicahuaxtla Triqui.
tsun: noun. Dated form of cun (“Chinese unit of length”).
tur: noun. Any of species Capra caucasica (West Caucasian tur) or Capra cylindricornis (East Caucasian tur of wild goats native to Caucasus.
urs: noun. The anniversary of the death of a Sufi saint, celebrated with music and ritual.
ursid: noun. Any species of the family Ursidae; a bear, a giant panda, or any of certain extinct relatives.
